title: phyllanthus brachyphyllus
intro: Phyllanthus brachyphyllus, also known as the West Indian gooseberry or Chien-Chieng, is a type of flowering plant in the family Phyllanthaceae. It is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Phyllanthus brachyphyllus include its ability to treat liver and kidney disorders, as well as its potential to lower blood sugar levels and reduce fever. The plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and its herbal preparations can also be found in the form of capsules or tinctures, which are used to harness its medicinal properties.
